title: Bags and bag-dispensing devices and methods of using same
abstract: Methods, apparatuses, and systems for dispensing and opening a bag to receive one or more objects. Some bag dispenser embodiments can comprise a pair of guiderails angled toward each other so that a bag positioned between the two guiderails can slide along guiderails to press open the mouth of bag. Other embodiments of the present disclosure comprise bags that are configured for use with the dispenser. Still other embodiments are described.
claims: Claims 1. A bag dispenser comprising: first and second guiderails spaced apart and parallel to each other, each guiderail comprising a supporting surface; a plurality of bags disposed between the guiderails, where the bag comprises opposing side panels defining a mouth and a foldable band coupled to an upper portion of one of the opposing side panels adjacent the mouth, the foldable band extending between the guiderails and having first and second ends supported by a corresponding one of the supporting surfaces of the guiderails, wherein the foldable band is configured such that once folded it will retain its folded orientation. 2. The bag dispenser of claim 1, further comprising a housing that defines an interior volume and an opening, where the first and second guiderails are disposed in the interior of the housing. 3. The bag dispenser of claim 2, wherein the housing comprises an inner sleeve and an outer sleeve, where the outer sleeve is configured to move relative to the inner sleeve and to circumscribe the inner sleeve to form the housing. 4. The bag dispenser of claim 3, wherein the first and second guiderails are integrated into or supported by the inner sleeve. 5. The bag dispenser of claim 3, wherein the outer sleeve comprises a lid configured for moving to cover and uncover the opening of the housing. 6. The bag dispenser of claim 1, wherein the foldable band comprises a wire extending between the first end and the second end. 7. The bag dispenser of claim 1, wherein the bag is configured to receive a feminine hygiene product. 8. A method of using the bag dispenser of claim 1, the method comprising removing one of the plurality of bags from the dispenser and placing a used hygiene product in the one of the plurality of bags and folding the foldable band to retain the used hygiene product therein. 9. A bag dispenser comprising: first and second guiderails spaced apart and each extending between a rear-end and a front-end, the first and second guiderails disposed such that a first distance between the guiderails at their rear-ends is greater than a second distance between the guiderails at at least one point between the rear-ends and the front-ends; and first and second stops, each disposed along one of the first and second guiderails, a third distance between the first and second stops being less than the first distance and the first and second stops configured to resist sliding movement of a bag supported by the guiderails. 10. The dispenser of claim 9, where each guiderail comprises a guiding surface and a supporting surface, where the guiding surface extends along the supporting surface and is configured to guide a bag as the bag is drawn toward the front ends of the guiderails. 11. The dispenser of claim 10, where the guiding surfaces are configured to compress a portion of the bag as the bag is drawn toward the front ends of the guiderails and the supporting surfaces are substantially coplanar. 12. The dispenser of claim 10 or 11, where each guiding surface extends above and lengthwise along a corresponding one of the supporting surface and is substantially perpendicular to the corresponding supporting surface. 13. The dispenser of any of claims 9-12, where at least one of the first and second stops comprises a recess in a corresponding one of the guiding surfaces. 14. The dispenser of claim 13, where each supporting surface extends at least partially into the recess of the corresponding one of the first and second stops. 15. The dispenser of any of claims 9-14, further comprising a housing that defines an interior volume and an opening, where the first and second guiderails are disposed in the interior of the housing. 16. The dispenser of any of claims 15, where the housing comprises a first sidewall, an opposing a second sidewall, a base opposite the opening, where the first and second guiderails are integrated into or unitary with the respective sidewalls. 17. The dispenser of any of claims 15 and 16, where the guiderails are disposed closer to the opening than the base. 18. The dispenser of any of claims 15 and 17, where the opening is dimensioned to allow a bag to pass through the opening at a location between the stops and the rear-ends of the guiderails. 19. The dispenser of any of claims 9-17, further comprising a bag disposed between the guiderails. 20. The dispenser of claim 19, where the bag comprises opposing side panels defining a mouth and semi-rigid bands coupled to upper portions of the opposing side panels adjacent the mouth, the bands extending between the guiderails and having first and second ends supported by a corresponding one of the supporting surfaces of the guiderails. 21. The dispenser of any of claims 20, where the third distance is small enough to prevent straightening of the bands and thereby to hold the mouth of the bag open. 22. The dispenser of any of claims 20 to 21, further comprising at least a first bag and a second bag disposed between the stops and the rear-ends such that an opposing panel of the first bag is facing an opposing panel of the second bag. 23. The dispenser of any of claims 20 to 22, where the opposing panels comprise a substantially nonabsorbent material. 24. The dispenser of any of claims 20-23, where the bag includes a fragrance. 25. The dispenser of any of claims 20-24, where the bag includes an antimicrobial agent. 26. The dispenser of claim 22, where the first bag is releasably coupled to the second bag. 27. The dispenser of claim 26, where the first bag is releasably coupled to the second bag by a releasable adhesive on at least a portion of outer facing surfaces of the bands. 28. The dispenser of claim 26, where the first bag is releasably coupled to the second bag by perforated member on each bag. 29. The dispenser of claim 9, where the guiderails curve toward each other. 30. The dispenser of claim 9, where the first and second guiderails are straight and define converging lines in a direction extending from the rear ends to the front ends. 31. The dispenser of claim 9, further comprising a housing defining an interior volume and comprising a first sidewall and an opposing a second sidewall, a rear-end wall and an opening opposite the rear-end wall, the opening dimensioned to allow a bag to pass therethrough. 32. A dispenser comprising: first and second curved guiderails converging such that a bag in a closed configuration may be placed at a starting position in the dispenser between the first and second guiderails and drawn into an open configuration as the bag slides along the guiderails from the starting position to an ending position between converging portions of the guiderails; and first and second stops, each disposed along one of the first and second guiderails and configured to resist sliding movement of the bag supported by the guiderails. 33. A method of opening a bag comprising: drawing a bag in a rear-to-front direction between two converging surfaces, the bag comprising opposing panels and defining a mouth, a front surface, an opposing rear surface, and two opposing lateral surfaces, wherein drawing the bag causes an inwardly facing force to be applied to each lateral surface of the bag and an outwardly facing force to be applied to the rear surface of the bag, thereby forcing the mouth to open. 34. The method of claim 33, further comprising drawing the bag in a front-to-rear or rear- to-front direction, thereby causing the mouth to return to a closed or more closed position. 35. The method of claim 33, placing a used hygiene product in the bag when the mouth is in the open position and/or sealing the mouth of the bag such that any contents in the bag are securely retained therein. 36. A method of closing a bag comprising: drawing a bag in a front-to-rear or rear-to-front direction between two converging surfaces, the bag comprising opposing panels and defining a mouth, a front surface, an opposing rear surface, and two opposing lateral surfaces, wherein drawing the bag in a rear-to-front or front-to-rear direction causes a reduction in an inwardly facing force applied to each lateral surface of the bag, thereby causing the mouth to close or be more closed. 37. The method of claim 36, placing a used hygiene product in the bag when the mouth is in the open position and/or sealing the mouth of the bag such that any contents in the bag are securely retained therein.
